tagline: Private bio-digital event intelligence for neuromorphic AI.
commonsPrinciple: If the architecture becomes useful for interfaces between biology, machines, and shared computational realities, it should remain free to inspect, copy, improve, and teach.
thesis: Use neuromorphic/event-driven sparsity to make homomorphic encrypted inference smaller and more practical, with Octra as a potential encrypted-compute integration layer.
recommendedClaim: Bio-digital signals stay local; neuromorphic systems make selected data sparse; FHE keeps selected computation private.
avoidClaim: Current neuromorphic chips directly run full FHE workloads as drop-in accelerators.

implementationBoundary.principle: JavaScript is a portable reference harness, not the low-level runtime target.
nativeHotPathTargets: native OpenFHE, SEAL, TFHE-rs, Concrete, or reviewed HE runtime
C, C++, Rust, Zig, DSP, FPGA, or edge implementation for signal encoding
native benchmark emission for latency, memory, ciphertext size, and energy per event window
implementationBoundary.rule: Do not use JavaScript toy timings for speed, energy, security, or production-runtime claims.

postQuantumTarget.principle: Quantum-resistant by design, cryptographically agile by default.
postQuantumTarget.implementationRule: Do not claim production quantum safety until concrete algorithms, parameter sets, libraries, side-channel posture, and review status are documented.
postQuantumTarget.nearTermArtifact: Add cryptoInventory to every benchmark output.

firstDemo.dataset: synthetic event stream, then N-MNIST or DVS-style event data

ci.scope: portable Node validation, JSON metadata parsing, placeholder scanning, and smoke artifact generation/upload
ci.nativeFhePolicy: OpenFHE and TFHE-rs native runs are release-gate/local checks unless a pinned heavy build environment is added.

canonicalEncoder.algorithm: integer threshold, electrode-to-spatial-bin lookup, per-unit refractory gate, bounded count accumulation
canonicalEncoder.caveat: Deterministic simulated sorter only; real neural sorting requires lawful data rights, calibration, validation, and hardware review.

currentPrototype.eventRepresentation: public active positions with encrypted active spike counts

linearModel.matrixOrientation: rows are classes, columns are flattened event features

privacyModeDecision.rule: Use public active positions only when exact metadata exposure is acceptable; use dense encrypted windows when active-position and sparsity metadata must be hidden.

packedVectorPlanning.defaultLane: BFV/BGV for non-negative integer spike counts
packedVectorPlanning.comparisonLane: CKKS for approximate real or fixed-point features after score-drift review

openFheCkksAdapter.useWhen: Prefer for approximate neural or ML feature scoring with real-valued inputs after score-drift and classification-margin review.

tfheRsAdapter.useWhen: Prefer for encrypted comparisons, threshold gates, small Boolean circuits, decision-tree or LUT-style sparse rules.

representationComparison.comparisonBasis: same synthetic raw neural frame, same linear score contract

currentPrototype.caveat: The current prototype is educational and not production cryptography.

gatewayPattern.principle: Sensitive signals stay local. Models receive only validated, transformed, permissioned event representations.
gatewayPattern.boundary: The local gateway is the only trusted component allowed to inspect raw signals.
gatewayPattern.encoderBoundary: raw neural-like intake is sorted locally into spatial-binned event windows before normalized gateway events are created
policyDefaults: safe actions must be local and reversible
gatewayPattern.caveat: Simulated gateway scaffold only. Do not use for medical, surveillance, coercive, external-control, or production cryptography claims without separate authorization and review.

whitepapers.title: Why We Need Encrypted Thoughts for Brain-Computer Interfaces
whitepapers.position: Encrypted thoughts is an architecture phrase: raw neural and biosignal streams stay local by default, selected event features are minimized and encrypted, external compute operates on ciphertext, and only authorized parties decrypt the result.
whitepapers.boundary: Non-medical, research-grade argument for BCI privacy architecture. Do not imply arbitrary thought reading, diagnosis, treatment, or production cryptographic assurance.

patentPackage.leadClaimFocus: spatial spike sorter -> permitted active positions -> encrypted active feature values -> BFVrns depth-1 scores = W x + bias scorer
patentPackage.status: provisional drafting package; not legal advice or filed-submission confirmation
patentPackage.reviewRule: Keep broad latent-compression language as fallback and lead with the concrete sorted sparse-event embodiment when patentability risk is the main concern.

architecture.encoder: binary spikes, spike counts, or low-bit event windows
architecture.crypto: CKKS, BFV/BGV, TFHE, or experimental HFHE path
architecture.octraLane: program/proof/settlement or future encrypted execution adapter
architecture.client: owns secret key and decrypts final result
